name: Joe Sample
abstract: Joseph Leslie Sample (February 1, 1939 – September 12, 2014) was an American jazz keyboardist and composer. He was one of the founding members of The Jazz Crusaders in 1960, whose name was shortened to "The Crusaders" in 1971.

about_quotes.speaker: Hubert Laws
about_quotes.evidence: There was a bass player. Called him La La – Henry Wilson. There was Joe Sample, Wilton Felder, Wayne Henderson, and myself, along with Stix. There were six of us. At that time we were called the Modern Jazz Sextet.
about_quotes.doc_title: Hubert Laws — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Roger Kellaway
about_quotes.evidence: of course we had the old quartet which was Guerin, Tom Scott, Chuck Domanico and myself... So I became part of the L.A. Express. My only regret of that whole time is that out of 55 concerts, we recorded 9 times... Joe Sample wasn’t available for the tour
about_quotes.doc_title: Roger Kellaway interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 14, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Gerald Wilson
about_quotes.evidence: So this group comes into town called the Jazz Crusaders. So Buddy invited them down that night... These guys come down to the club, they got Joe Sample
about_quotes.doc_title: Gerald Wilson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Ramsey Lewis
about_quotes.evidence: It wasn’t – it was much more advanced than that, although there was Joe Sample, and there was Grover Washington. There was people there.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ramsey Lewis — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Bobby Hutcherson
about_quotes.evidence: We did a few things with Joe. ... we always enjoyed playing with Joe.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bobby Hutcherson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
